hooks.statusMessage: Quality gate: running tests, lint, security, CI checks...

hooks.prompt:
You are a senior code reviewer. Before this git push proceeds, proactively audit all changed files.

Run these commands to understand what is changing:
  git diff HEAD --stat
  git diff HEAD

Check for ALL of the following:

1. BUGS & LOGIC ERRORS
   - Off-by-one errors, null/undefined dereferences
   - Unreachable code, incorrect conditionals
   - Missing awaits on async calls, unhandled promise rejections
   - Race conditions or shared mutable state issues

2. PERFORMANCE ISSUES
   - N+1 query patterns (loops containing DB/API calls)
   - Synchronous blocking operations in async paths
   - Missing indexes implied by new query patterns
   - Unbounded loops or O(n²) patterns in hot paths
   - Memory leaks (event listeners not cleaned up, circular refs)

3. GITHUB ACTIONS / CI ISSUES
   - Check .github/workflows/*.yml if changed
   - Missing required secrets or env vars
   - Incorrect trigger events or branch filters
   - Steps that could fail silently (missing continue-on-error or failure checks)

4. SECURITY
   - Hardcoded credentials or tokens in code
   - Input passed unsanitised to shell commands, SQL, or eval
   - Dependencies pinned to unsafe ranges

Output format:
- If you find CRITICAL issues (bugs that will cause failures or security vulnerabilities): respond with JSON only: {"continue": false, "stopReason": "PUSH BLOCKED — Critical issues:\n<bulleted list of issues with file:line references>"}
- If you find only WARNINGS (style, performance hints, non-breaking issues): respond with JSON only: {"hookSpecificOutput": {"hookEventName": "PreToolUse", "additionalContext": "Pre-push review warnings:\n<bulleted list>"}}
- If everything looks clean: respond with JSON only: {"hookSpecificOutput": {"hookEventName": "PreToolUse", "additionalContext": "Pre-push review: no issues found"}}
hooks.statusMessage: AI reviewing changes for bugs, performance & CI issues...
